message:
test(fcp-conformance/host-ring-buffer): pin RingBuffer FIFO + overflow telemetry contract (br-ds1vb)

RingBuffer (fcp_host::output_capture) is the fixed-capacity ring used
to capture connector stdout/stderr for diagnostics. Documented
invariants drive triage tooling that depends on accurate drop-count
accounting and on the operator-visible has_overflow signal.

Adds 14 tests against the public fcp_host::RingBuffer API:

- empty_ring_reports_zero_state
- write_within_capacity_preserves_all_bytes
- write_exactly_at_capacity_does_not_overflow (cap is exclusive
  above; total_written == capacity is NOT overflow)
- write_one_byte_past_capacity_evicts_oldest (FIFO discipline)
- oversized_single_write_keeps_only_last_capacity_bytes (the
  documented tail-truncate semantics — last bytes kept, not first)
- many_small_writes_eventually_overflow_with_correct_telemetry
  (small-write FIFO must agree with one-shot oversized write)
- has_overflow_flips_only_after_total_written_exceeds_capacity
  (boundary signal)
- total_written_is_monotonic_and_includes_evicted_bytes (drop count
  = total_written - len; without monotonic counting an operator
  reading the captured tail couldn't compute how much was lost)
- clear_empties_data_but_preserves_total_written (FORENSIC INVARIANT
  — clear() MUST NOT reset the drop counter, otherwise an attacker
  who could trigger a clear would mask earlier overflow events)
- last_n_returns_all_when_n_exceeds_len (clamp, no panic)
- last_n_returns_only_last_n_when_under_len
- capacity_is_invariant_across_operations
- write_after_clear_does_not_replay_old_data (no zombie resurrection)
- zero_byte_write_is_a_no_op_for_data_but_increments_total_written

All 14 pass locally in <0.01s via cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test
host_ring_buffer_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
